# `ui/api.ts` — API Reference

## Overview

The `ui/api.ts` module provides a collection of asynchronous functions for interacting with the Todo API. It covers the core CRUD operations for managing todo items: listing, creating, toggling completion state, and deleting.

---

## Functions

### `listTodos`

Fetches all existing todo items.

```ts
import { listTodos } from '...'
```

**Parameters**

None.

**Returns:** `Promise<Todo[]>`

Resolves with an array of `Todo` objects.

**Example**

```ts
const todos = await listTodos();
console.log(todos); // Todo[]
```

---

### `createTodo`

Creates a new todo item with the given title.

```ts
import { createTodo } from '...'
```

**Parameters**

| Name    | Type     | Description                          |
|---------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| `title` | `string` | The title for the new todo item. |

**Returns:** `Promise<Todo>`

Resolves with the newly created `Todo` object.

**Example**

```ts
const newTodo = await createTodo("Buy groceries");
console.log(newTodo); // Todo
```

---

### `toggleTodo`

Updates the completion state of an existing todo item.

```ts
import { toggleTodo } from '...'
```

**Parameters**

| Name        | Type      | Description                                      |
|-------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `id`        | `string`  | The unique identifier of the todo to update.     |
| `completed` | `boolean` | The new completion state to set on the todo item. |

**Returns:** `Promise<Todo>`

Resolves with the updated `Todo` object.

**Example**

```ts
const updatedTodo = await toggleTodo("abc123", true);
console.log(updatedTodo); // Todo
```

---

### `deleteTodo`

Deletes an existing todo item by its ID.

```ts
import { deleteTodo } from '...'
```

**Parameters**

| Name | Type     | Description                                    |
|------|----------|------------------------------------------------|
| `id` | `string` | The unique identifier of the todo to delete. |

**Returns:** `Promise<void>`

Resolves with no value upon successful deletion.

**Example**

```ts
await deleteTodo("abc123");
```
